content.introduction: Inflation reduces purchasing power, making everyday goods more expensive, which can strain household budgets and impact long-term savings. Persistently higher prices also stress consumers, affecting both physical and mental health. The correlation between inflation rates and expectations continues to influence economic decisions.
body: While the pace of rising inflation is slowing, persistently higher prices continue to weigh on consumers and policymakers alike.
